rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop); no Cowork for AE101.

rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No speculative capability claims in student-facing body. Maintainer-block claims are consistent with shipped Claude Code behavior per compendium records.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Maintain living capability notes; consult before asserting.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer claim 'Skills install to ~/.claude/skills/ (user-level), auto-discover in every session regardless of cwd' is consistent with known Claude Code behavior for user-level skills and does not conflict with rule 6d (which covers CLAUDE.md auto-load, a separate mechanism).

Maintainer block's 'auto-discover in every session' refers to skill auto-discovery, a different mechanism from CLAUDE.md filename-based auto-load. No student-facing claims about auto-load. N/A in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 23: 'Open a new session with the repo as the working directory.' Uses correct session-unit language.

<CONTENT_URL> appears in body prose (line 29), not inside a fenced block. Documented exception in maintainer block (line 68): build-time substituted by scripts/build-workbook.js. The 'URL:' open-hook in ae101-prework-download-tarball is a valid rule 1d open-hook — student must supply the customer-specific URL that Claude cannot know.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All three prompts have lead-ins: '**3.b. Or have Claude download it for you.** Ask Claude to fetch the content tarball…'; 'Ask Claude to extract the tarball, install the curated skills…'; 'If Step 4's screening feels like Claude is asking everything at once…'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: ae101-prework-extract-and-install: 'Install them wherever personal Claude Code skills belong on this machine.' Deliberately path-agnostic — correct per rule 4.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No bold/italic markdown found inside any of the three fenced prompt bodies.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Preamble before action — structural trigger: 4+ named sub-steps or plan mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: ae101-prework-extract-and-install (5 sub-steps): first sentence is 'Walk these in order, ask one question at a time if you need to, no preamble.'
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Suppressor is at the TOP of the prompt, not as the canonical last-line form ('No preamble. <first action>' as the prompt's last line per rule 17a). Functionally present but non-canonical shape. TODO: move to last line for strict canonical compliance. Non-blocking.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in the registry; module files reference by marker.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All three prompts exist in registry: ae101-prework-download-tarball.md, ae101-prework-extract-and-install.md, ae101-prework-one-at-a-time.md. Module file uses {{prompt:…}} markers. Correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No module-name references in prompt prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: ae101-prework-extract-and-install contains 'Module 1' in 'confirm the repo is ready for Module 1' — readiness-target colloquial use, not a curriculum-internal identifier the agent must pattern-match. Not a path, branch, or commit reference. Non-blocking.
No research citations, practitioner claims, or statistics in this file. Prework is setup-only; research-claims rules are N/A.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism — every named tool, skill, or path must exist.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: access-control-analysis exists at ~/.claude/skills/access-control-analysis/. stride exists at ~/.claude/skills/stride/. agentic-nerd exists at ~/.claude/skills/agentic-nerd/ (mentioned in maintainer block only). All three install targets are real shipped skills.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body prose around prompts describes steps, not mechanics. The forcing function for question-dump handling is in the prompt itself ('ask one question at a time if you need to') and in the optional steering prompt ae101-prework-one-at-a-time. Correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prework does not build verification machinery — N/A.
Prework is setup-only; context-tier teaching belongs in M1+. N/A here.

platform_claims_checked.claim: CLI or desktop, your call. Open a new session with the repo as the working directory.
Claude will need to write outside the repo (~/.claude/skills/); approve the permission prompts when they fire.
Skills install to ~/.claude/skills/ (user-level), auto-discover in every session regardless of cwd. [maintainer block]

urls_sampled.note: Example URL from maintainer block build-time-substitution note. Only URL in the file; no student-facing HTTP URLs to sample (CONTENT_URL is build-time substituted at render).

todos.note: Preamble suppressor 'no preamble' is at the START of the 5-sub-step prompt, not as the canonical last-line form required by rule 17a ('No preamble. <first action>' as the prompt's last line). Functionally present; canonical shape is last-line. Non-blocking TODO.
